seoTitle: Long Jetty vs Long Plain — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Long Jetty and Long Plain. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Long Jetty vs Long Plain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Long Jetty and Long Plain.
heading: Long Jetty vs Long Plain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Long Jetty, New South Wales and Long Plain, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Long Jetty has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Long Plain (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Long Jetty or Long Plain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Long Jetty has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Long Plain scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Long Jetty scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Long Plain scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Long Jetty or Long Plain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Long Jetty has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
